title: Python: EDHOC signature verification may use wrong public key format
description:
In python/src/lichen/crypto/edhoc.py:162-166, signature verification was added:

    verify_key = VerifyKey(peer_pubkey)
    try:
        verify_key.verify(m_2, signature_2)
    except Exception as e:
        raise ValueError(f'Signature verification failed: {e}') from e

The VerifyKey from nacl expects an Ed25519 public key (32 bytes). However, peer_pubkey is passed in from the EDHOC exchange and its format depends on the CRED_R structure. The code assumes CRED_R = raw pubkey, but RFC 9528 allows various credential types.

Location: python/src/lichen/crypto/edhoc.py:162-166
Risk: Signature verification may fail or accept incorrect signatures if peer_pubkey format doesn't match expectations.
Fix: Document the expected peer_pubkey format and validate it before creating VerifyKey.
